title: Mushy Syrian Lentils Curry
description: A herbal, gooey curry featuring massaman paste, simmered lentils and a dash of Mediterranean flair.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add the massaman curry paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes to release flavors.
Toss in diced carrot, potato, and rinsed lentils, coating them with the spice mixture.
Pour in coconut milk and vegetable broth, then add ground cumin, coriander, turmeric, cardamom, and dried mint; stir well.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and let simmer for 25 minutes until lentils are soft and the sauce thickens into a gooey consistency.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then fluff the lentils with a wooden spoon.
Transfer to serving bowls and garnish with fresh cilantro leaves.
Serve hot with warm flatbread or rice.
